Britain was almost destroyed, and would have been if not for the aid of Montor’s newly founded order of warrior priests that had noticed an increase of undead activity in the area prior to the onslaught. The Mad Duke was slain, as well as his entire bloodline. But the Necromancer would not lose such a useful pawn, and it is said that the Duke can still be seen within his abandoned manor, and sometimes seen delivering messages (or killings) from the Dark Dragon Cult that once deceived him. The Duke might be the Necromancer’s favorite slave, and killing him might lead one to the cult’s hiding place. Though none of the Necromancer’s minions stay dead for long.

Book.title: Darkest Darkness Vol III of III
Book.author: Garamon the Wizard
Book.Text:
The Void is an endless expanse of darkness and unknowable monsters, but our realm and theirs are not entirely different. All monsters, good or bad, can trace their origins to the influence of the Void. As all things once came from that black abyss. However, the two planes of existence do not mix, and only the gods can travel between the veil. It is rumored though, that the place where the Dark Dragon once crossed into our world still has a small tear in reality that can lead back to the world of darkness should one be mad enough to attempt it. This deep place lies in the center of the darkest lake far beneath the light of the stars, and is known to be protected by a great and terrible structure that no mortal hand had a part in constructing. Fevered whispers of men long mad speak of this place as the Black Pyramid, heart of the Necromancer’s power, and birthplace of the most frightening monsters. A strange key bars all entry, save to the Necromancer, who alone knows the way.

Seeking such a place is impossible to mortals, but to a titan it could perhaps hold the greatest challenge or some dark unknowable treasures. And even if one could brave the depths of the pyramid, entering the portal to the Void is a feat no single entity could accomplish. It would take the might and knowledge of many titans to enter the abyss. But all of these secrets are best left forgotten deep below the world. Where no light can expose its dangers.

Book.title: Immortal King’s Challenge
Book.author: Milydor the Sage
Book.Text:
The first ruler of Britain and holder of the Gem of Immortality, King Amadeus Wolfgang, was a great warrior in life and believed wholeheartedly that the strength of heroes triumphed over more conventional armies and tactics. So much so that he handled most threats to the kingdom himself, or hired great adventurers to do so. In his final years, Wolfgang set in motion plans to strengthen his bloodline, so that great heroes would rise in future generations to protect the world. Many powerful adventurers owe their innate strength to Wolfgang’s efforts. As the great king lay dying however, he discovered the consequences of bearing the Gem of Immortality for so many years were that he could not die a natural death. Wishing to avoid becoming an undead tyrant, he faked his burial and instead constructed a vast tomb complex beneath the city, with hopes to challenge the greatest of his bloodline and strengthen their powers should they come to seek him out. He took with him his Honorguard and servants, who happily followed him into the fate that awaited their now undead king. Now hidden from the notice of history, his royal court still influences the living in subtle ways. And any that search below are met with fierce challenge, but great treasures await any that can survive Wolfgang’s various trials, and prove to him that they are of his bloodline. Finding his tomb is simple enough, there are many passages in the ancient ruins beneath the city, though the quickest route is to follow the king’s footsteps, and look beneath his forgotten tomb.

Within his crypt, Wolfgang separated his domain into four distinct sections. As he intended to test the strength of future heroes and not kill them outright, the zones are each of different difficulty, though each has a magic word that must be spoken on square tiles at the end that are based on the trials leading up to them. The words are each two parts, and the clues are within the zones on the way to the treasure room. They must be spoken on the brightly colored tiles or you will find no reward for your efforts. Wolfgang decreed these four sections be labeled the “stone”, “bronze”, “silver”, and “gold” rooms. The stone area is the lesser guarded, and entered from the first doorway to the left down the grand hall. This place was designed with new heroes in mind, and is the easiest of the trials. The bronze rooms are like a maze, and are entered immediately to the right in the grand hall. The silver and gold areas of the crypt were made only for the strongest of adventurers, and should be treated as such. They are each at the end of the great hall. The immortal form of King Wolfgang himself guards the golden quarters alongside his now undead Honorguard. And he welcomes the chance to test other’s prowess in battle. The immortal king did not trust only his own strength to defend his treasures, however, and enlisted the aid of great magical creatures to test each would-be looter of his prized treasure rooms. These beasts are each summoned when an explorer enters a treasure room, and it is actually the same magic word used to enter each one that also summons the guardian. Each of these creatures carries a unique treasure in addition to anything left behind by the Immortal King, so if your strength matches your ambition: seek the magic words, defeat the summoned guardians, and claim your prize.

Book.title: Ultimate Weapon
Book.author: Erethian the Mage
Book.Text:
Britain was not always the seat of power of the civilized kingdoms, it was once known as Ordain, as it was ordained by the gods to be a safehaven for the righteous. King Wolfgang himself established this once great kingdom. Now it lies in ruins, and the landmass that once supported it is now mostly below sea level. In its prime, no threat was too great to threaten its walls, until a terrible darkness from the Void itself saw its way into the world. Many heroes met their end as the darkness approached. The royal advisor, a mysterious wizard known as Dio, looked to magic and knowledge to combat this foe while King Wolfgang still believed in the strength of heroes. A great stone fortress was built, with strange technology channeling the forces of magic within its walls. Experiments to create an ultimate weapon ensued deep within the keep, with many monsters being captured and brought inside. In the end no monster was deemed powerful enough to face the monster of the Void, and even if there was, Dio concluded that any creature would be too strong to control. And thus the monsters were sealed away in magical stasis, forever imprisoned within the stone walls.

It would seem a monster would not become the ultimate weapon, but instead one born of magic and technology. However, the forces of the magic castle would not have time to test their research, as the darkness continued to consume the land and the King’s champions were all but defeated. Only days away from losing completely to the encroaching darkness, Dio unleashed the magic within the castle from a massive device, and Ordain was blown away. The tides washed over the ruins of the city, and the great evil from the Void was sent back from whence it came… Survivors of the ordeal told stories of how the world was saved, but the city would never recover. The magical fortress was sealed away so the mistakes of the past could not be repeated, but the wizard was too cautious to destroy the weapon outright. And thus it remains, fully capable of its ancient act of destruction, though one would have to speak the forgotten words of passage to once again enter the bastion. Some speculate that the libraries that survived the blast beneath the ground of Ordain itself might still contain the knowledge to breach the magical seal, though even if one did discover the key, entering the fortress would be quite dangerous. As Dio’s monsters are still magically sealed within, and with the same malice and savagery as their day of capture. If one was to break inside and best the monsters however, Dio’s ultimate weapon of power would be at their hands, should they learn how to activate it. Let us hope such power will never be needed again…

Book.title: Order of Righteousness
Book.author: Darun the Priest
Book.Text: In a world of monsters, undead, and evil doers, the most righteous of men must stand up and protect the weak. In an effort to rally together against the darkness, many holy paladins joined as one under a common banner and began a crusade to make the world a safer place. In Sosaria, they established a headquarters where they were needed most: the dark land of Montor, recently ravaged by an evil ruler and left open to attack. With the crusaders help, the civilized folk were able to reclaim their seat of power and safeguard their people once again. Now the bastion of light stands proud within the forest of Montor, sending out its agents of light wherever they are needed most. Within its walls, the finest champions and priests learn their craft, trained by the best teachers of the realm. Deep beneath the cathedral, wise priests collect knowledge to better wage war against the darkness, and divine creatures offer training and challenges to hone the blades of the righteous. To reach these halls, one must seek out the forest of Montor and find the locked gate. Speak the mantra of the crusaders and you will be allowed entry. But be prepared if you are to delve into the temples below, the trials are only for the mightiest of champions, not beginners seeking meager treasures.

Book.title: Trial of Titans
Book.author: Xavier the Theurgist
Book.Text: With the creation of the Obelisk and the Titans, a tower was constructed on the island of Umber Veil to test their power and magical prowess. Each level contains illusions of great monsters to challenge even the strongest of champions! Should one attain titanhood, seek the three steps towards your journey to ascension for a clue to the magic phrase needed to enter the tower. The first being the key to the Underworld, and the second where your quest to titanhood truly began. Lastly return to the Obelisk to assemble the clues, and speak them at the tower’s door. Within face the trials of the Rune Knight, and claim your rightful place as a true titan of ether.

Book.title: The Greatest Foe
Book.author: Dreova of the Isles
Book.Text: The strongest golem ever created, Exodus the Destroyer, was thought to be slain years ago. However, it was discovered that no mortal means can permanently undo the enchantments that cause the creature to simply rebuild itself after each defeat. Indeed, the golem only becomes stronger with each battle, and will forever roam the realm seeking greater knowledge within the dungeons beneath the world. There is however a place that it goes to reassemble itself, the very forge in which it was made. Deep below the castle for which it is named, Exodus gathers its strength for an unobtainable mission: to become the most powerful creature in all of existence. The Exodus one might encounter out in the world is but a prototype of its true potential, with its full might only on display in its place of creation. Where it will truly fight to the end to protect its precious instruments of creation. Getting to its lair is simple enough, one must follow the creature to its summoning circle and speak the creature’s name and final potential, “Final Exodus” to descend into the creature’s domain. Should you ever use the contents of this book to challenge such a machine in its prime, be prepared. And do not go alone. Exodus in its true form is perhaps the greatest foe ever to walk the mortal world.

Book.title: Sunken City
Book.author: Sentri the Seeker
Book.Text: Atlantis is a myth as old as time, but there is truth to the matter… Poseidon once blessed a city on the sea with great bounty, though they offended the gods by declaring that they had become greater, and thus they were cast down into the dark depths. Only by praying to sinister forces did they survive, corrupting themselves into becoming more monster than man. And so the city lies at the bottom of the sea of Sosaria, filled with sunken treasures and the once great inhabitants, now slaves to a dark master’s will. To find such a place, one must seek the cavern of the deep and cast a long forgotten spell to descend to the ocean’s floor. Such an incantation could only be learned by those alive at the time. Seek thee the titans, only they could know the ancient words.

Book.title: The Wishmaster
Book.author: Risa the Scholar
Book.Text:
It is said that a great wizard once defeated a terrible foe in the distant past, but lost his humanity in the process. He vanished from history and left this realm behind, becoming a god of mystery. Forever barred from participating in life’s affairs, but still bound to the mortal world. As he was now unable to influence fate, and still regretting the actions of his mortal self, the god turned to a more subtle existence. No longer would he take matters into his own hands, now he exists only to aid in deciding the fate of others. He began to carve out new planes of existence in the Void, tunneling into the fabric of reality and creating many strange and mysterious dungeons. Many of which lead directly to his seat of power, the only place where he can be directly summoned by mortal creatures. There he is inclined to grant a single wish to whatever can make it to his inner sanctum, though the wishes always come with a price. And thus the god was named the Wishmaster by many scholars who heard tell of the wishes adventurers were granted, though the wizards true name may be lost forever. Nevertheless, if wishes be what you seek, go to the dungeons not marked on any map, and solve the riddles to reach the end. Many paths lead to the Wishmaster, though perhaps the easiest one lies within the Serpent Isles near a remote castle.

If one is lucky enough to be granted a wish, the price paid is always up front. The Wishmaster demands a single sacrifice of great importance, such as a legendary artifact or powerful tamed beast, before granting any wish. The wish can be for anything, though typically the more outrageous a wish, the further from the request is actually granted. If you are to seek out this power and make a wish for yourself, be respectful, speak directly, and keep your request simple. Many adventurers lost their lives to the folly of angering a god of wishes and mystery. Though many still found their dreams answered, by making a wise wish.
